length.en: the measurement of something from one end to the other

ex.en: The length of the room is five meters.

length.en: the amount of time that something lasts

ex.en: The length of the movie was almost three hours.
ex.ko: 그 영화의 길이는 거의 세 시간이었다.

lessen.en: make something smaller in size, amount, or degree

ex.en: Exercise can lessen the risk of heart disease.
ex.ko: 운동은 심장병의 위험을 줄일 수 있다.

lettuce.en: a leafy green vegetable eaten raw, especially in salads

ex.en: She added some lettuce to the sandwich.
He called her a liar in front of everyone.
ex.ko: 그는 모두 앞에서 그녀를 거짓말쟁이라고 불렀다.

liberate.en: set someone or something free from oppression or control

ex.en: The army liberated the city from occupation.

liberate.en: release gas or energy as a result of a chemical reaction

ex.en: The reaction liberates a large amount of heat.

liberation.en: the act of being set free from oppression or control

ex.en: The country celebrated its liberation from colonial rule.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 식민 통치로부터의 해방을 축하했다.

librarian.en: a person who works in or manages a library

ex.en: The librarian helped me find the book.
ex.ko: 그 사서는 내가 책을 찾도록 도와주었다.

lifeguard.en: a person employed to watch over swimmers and prevent accidents

ex.en: The lifeguard rescued a swimmer from the waves.
ex.ko: 그 안전요원은 파도에서 수영하던 사람을 구조했다.

lifelong.en: continuing for all of a person's life

ex.en: They have been lifelong friends since childhood.

lift.en: raise something to a higher position

ex.en: He lifted the heavy box onto the shelf.
ex.ko: 그는 그 무거운 상자를 들어 선반 위에 올렸다.

ex.en: Can you give me a lift to the station?
ex.ko: 역까지 좀 태워다 줄 수 있어?

lift.en: a machine that carries people up and down between floors of a building (British English for elevator)

ex.en: We took the lift to the tenth floor.

lighter.en: a small device used to produce a flame, especially for lighting cigarettes

ex.en: He used a lighter to light his cigarette.
She tapped him lightly on the shoulder.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그의 어깨를 가볍게 톡톡 두드렸다.

ex.en: He didn't take the warning lightly.
ex.ko: 그는 그 경고를 가볍게 여기지 않았다.

lightning.en: a sudden flash of light in the sky during a storm

ex.en: Lightning struck the old tree.

likeness.en: the quality of being similar to someone or something else

ex.en: There's a strong likeness between the twins.
ex.ko: 그 쌍둥이 사이에는 강한 닮음이 있다.

likeness.en: a picture or image that looks like a particular person

ex.en: The painting is a good likeness of her.
ex.ko: 그 그림은 그녀와 꽤 닮은 초상이다.

limit.en: the greatest amount or level of something that is allowed or possible

ex.en: There is a speed limit on this road.

limit.en: keep something within a certain amount, level, or range

ex.en: You should limit your screen time.

limitation.cue: 할 수 있는 범위를 제한하는 요소
limitation.en: a restriction on what someone or something can do

ex.en: Everyone has their own limitations.

limited.en: restricted in amount, extent, or scope

ex.en: We have limited time to finish the project.
ex.ko: 우리는 그 프로젝트를 끝낼 시간이 제한되어 있다.

limp.en: walk with difficulty because one leg or foot is hurt

ex.en: He limped off the field after the injury.
ex.ko: 그는 부상을 당한 후 절뚝거리며 경기장을 떠났다.

limp.en: soft and without any firmness or energy

ex.en: The flowers looked limp after a week without water.
ex.ko: 그 꽃들은 일주일간 물을 주지 않아 축 늘어져 보였다.

link.en: connect two or more things or ideas together

ex.en: Scientists linked the disease to poor diet.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 그 질병을 부실한 식습관과 관련지었다.

link.en: a connection on a website that takes you to another page when clicked

ex.en: Click the link to see more photos.
ex.ko: 더 많은 사진을 보려면 그 링크를 클릭하세요.

link.en: a connection between two things or people

ex.en: There's a clear link between exercise and health.
ex.ko: 운동과 건강 사이에는 분명한 연관이 있다.

liquid.en: a substance that flows freely, like water

ex.en: Water is a liquid at room temperature.

liquid.en: in the form of a liquid

ex.en: The medicine comes in liquid form.
She has a great interest in literary works.
ex.ko: 그녀는 문학 작품에 큰 관심이 있다.

literature.en: written works, such as novels and poems, valued for their artistic quality

ex.en: She studied English literature at university.

literature.en: published information on a particular subject

ex.en: He reviewed the scientific literature on climate change.
ex.ko: 그는 기후 변화에 관한 과학 문헌을 검토했다.

liver.en: a large organ in the body that cleans the blood and helps digest food

ex.en: Too much alcohol can damage your liver.

liver.en: the liver of an animal, eaten as food

ex.en: He doesn't like the taste of liver.
ex.ko: 그는 간 요리의 맛을 좋아하지 않는다.

loaf.en: bread shaped and baked as a single whole piece

ex.en: She bought a loaf of bread at the bakery.
ex.ko: 그녀는 빵집에서 빵 한 덩이를 샀다.

ex.en: He just loafed around the house all weekend.
ex.ko: 그는 주말 내내 그냥 집에서 빈둥거렸다.

locate.en: find the exact position of something or someone

ex.en: We used GPS to locate the missing hiker.
ex.ko: 우리는 GPS로 실종된 등산객의 위치를 찾았다.

locate.en: be situated in a particular place

ex.en: The hotel is located near the airport.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 공항 근처에 위치해 있다.

irregular.en: not happening at regular times or following a regular pattern

ex.en: She has an irregular sleep schedule.
ex.ko: 그녀는 불규칙한 수면 습관을 갖고 있다.

irregular.en: not even or regular in shape or arrangement

ex.en: The rock has an irregular shape.
His constant complaining irritates me.
ex.ko: 그의 끊임없는 불평은 나를 짜증나게 한다.

irritate.en: cause a part of the body to become sore or inflamed

ex.en: The smoke irritated her eyes.
ex.ko: 그 연기는 그녀의 눈을 따갑게 했다.

isle.en: a small island (used especially in literature or place names)

ex.en: They dreamed of living on a tropical isle.
ex.ko: 그들은 열대의 섬에서 사는 것을 꿈꿨다.

isolation.en: the state of being separated from other people or things

ex.en: The pandemic increased feelings of isolation.

isolation.en: a country's separation from international politics or relations

ex.en: The country faced growing international isolation.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 점점 커지는 국제적 고립에 직면했다.

ivory.en: the hard white substance that forms the tusks of elephants

ex.en: The statue was carved from ivory.
She painted the walls ivory.

jade.en: a hard green stone used to make jewelry and ornaments

ex.en: She wore a necklace made of jade.

jade.en: a light green color like that of jade

ex.en: The dress was a beautiful shade of jade.

jail.en: a place where criminals are kept as punishment

ex.en: He spent five years in jail.
She was jailed for fraud.

jar.en: a glass container with a wide opening, used for storing food

ex.en: She opened a jar of jam.

jar.en: have an unpleasant or disturbing effect on someone

ex.en: The loud noise jarred her nerves.
ex.ko: 그 시끄러운 소리는 그녀의 신경을 거슬리게 했다.

jazz.en: a style of music with strong rhythms, often with improvisation, that originated among African Americans

ex.en: He loves listening to jazz on weekends.
ex.ko: 그는 주말에 재즈 듣는 것을 좋아한다.

jealous.en: feeling unhappy because someone else has something you want

ex.en: She was jealous of her sister's new car.

jealous.en: feeling worried or angry because you think someone is trying to take a person you love

ex.en: He gets jealous whenever she talks to other guys.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀가 다른 남자와 이야기할 때마다 질투한다.

jewel.en: a precious stone used in jewelry

ex.en: The crown was covered in jewels.
She jogs in the park every morning.
This photo might jog your memory.
ex.ko: 이 사진이 네 기억을 되살릴 수도 있어.

jogging.en: the activity of running slowly and steadily for exercise

ex.en: Jogging is a great way to stay fit.

joint.en: a part of the body where two bones meet and can bend, such as the knee or elbow

ex.en: He has pain in his knee joint.

joint.en: shared by or involving two or more people

ex.en: They opened a joint bank account.

journal.en: a written record of your thoughts and daily experiences

ex.en: She keeps a journal of her travels.

journal.en: a magazine or publication dealing with a particular subject, especially an academic one

ex.en: His research was published in a medical journal.

journalist.en: a person who writes for newspapers, magazines, or news websites

judgment.en: an opinion or decision made after careful thought

judgment.en: an official decision made by a court of law

ex.en: The court delivered its judgment yesterday.

judgment.en: the ability to make sensible decisions

ex.en: She has good judgment when it comes to hiring people.

jug.en: a container with a handle used for holding and pouring liquids

The peach was ripe and juicy.
ex.ko: 그 복숭아는 잘 익고 즙이 많았다.

juicy.en: (of a story or piece of information) exciting or interesting, often because it involves scandal

ex.ko: 그녀는 이웃에 대한 흥미진진한 소문을 나에게 말해줬다.

jumper.en: a knitted garment covering the upper body, worn over a shirt (British English for sweater)

ex.en: She wore a warm wool jumper in winter.
ex.ko: 그녀는 겨울에 따뜻한 울 스웨터를 입었다.

jungle.en: a thick tropical forest with many trees and plants growing close together

ex.en: They got lost deep in the jungle.
ex.ko: 그들은 정글 깊은 곳에서 길을 잃었다.

jungle.en: a place or situation of fierce struggle and competition

ex.en: The business world can be a real jungle.
ex.ko: 비즈니스 세계는 정말 약육강식의 세계일 수 있다.

justice.en: fairness in the way people are treated

ex.en: They fought for justice and equality.

justice.en: a judge, especially in a high court

ex.en: She was appointed as a Supreme Court Justice.

kettle.en: a container used for boiling water, usually with a spout and handle

ex.en: She put the kettle on to make tea.

keyboard.en: a set of keys used to type on a computer or typewriter

ex.en: He typed the report on his keyboard.

keyboard.en: the row of keys on a piano or similar musical instrument

ex.en: She plays the keyboard in a band.

killing.en: the act of causing someone's death

ex.en: The killing shocked the whole town.
ex.ko: 그 살인 사건은 온 마을을 충격에 빠뜨렸다.

killing.en: a very large profit made quickly

ex.en: He made a killing on the stock market.

kindness.en: the quality of being generous, helpful, and caring towards others

ex.en: She treated everyone with kindness.

kindness.en: a kind act done for someone

ex.en: Thank you for your many kindnesses.
The kitten played with a ball of yarn.
ex.ko: 그 새끼 고양이는 실뭉치를 갖고 놀았다.

knit.en: make clothing or fabric by looping yarn together with needles

knit.en: draw your eyebrows together, especially when worried or thinking hard

lab.en: a room or building used for scientific experiments and research

laboratory.en: a room or building equipped for scientific research or experiments

ladder.en: a piece of equipment with steps used for climbing up or down

ladder.en: a series of stages by which someone can advance in a career or society

ex.en: She worked her way up the corporate ladder.
The lamb followed its mother across the field.

lamb.en: meat from a young sheep, eaten as food

landlord.en: a person who owns a house or apartment and rents it to others

ex.en: She paid rent to her landlord every month.
The success was largely due to teamwork.

latecomer.en: someone who arrives after the expected time

ex.ko: 지각한 사람들은 밖에서 기다려달라는 요청을 받았다.

ex.en: Have you seen him lately?

laughter.en: the sound or action of laughing

ex.en: The room was filled with laughter.

launch.en: introduce a new product or service to the public for the first time

ex.ko: 그 회사는 이번 주에 새 스마트폰을 출시했다.

launch.en: the act of launching a product, event, or spacecraft

ex.en: Owning a gun is lawful in some countries.

lay.en: put something down carefully in a flat or horizontal position

layer.en: a single thickness of material covering a surface or lying between other things

ex.en: The cake has three layers of chocolate.
ex.ko: 그 케이크는 세 겹의 초콜릿 층으로 되어 있다.

leadership.en: the ability to lead a group of people

leadership.en: the group of people who lead an organization

ex.ko: 그 회사의 지도부는 새로운 계획을 발표했다.

leading.en: most important or advanced in a particular field

league.en: a group of sports teams that compete against each other

ex.en: His team won the league this season.
ex.ko: 그의 팀은 이번 시즌 리그에서 우승했다.

league.en: an association of countries, organizations, or people joined for a common purpose

ex.en: The League of Nations was formed after World War I.

lean.en: bend or tilt your body against something for support

ex.en: She leaned against the wall.

lean.en: (of a person) thin and fit, without extra fat

ex.en: He has a lean, athletic build.
ex.ko: 그는 마르고 탄탄한 체격을 가지고 있다.

lean.en: containing little fat, or having little excess

leap.en: jump a long way, or jump up high

leap.en: a sudden large increase in something

lecture.en: a talk given to teach people about a subject

lecture.en: a long talk in which someone criticizes or warns you about something

ex.ko: 나는 늦은 것에 대해 엄마한테 잔소리를 들었다.

ex.ko: 그들은 결혼식을 위해 아름다운 장소를 선택했다.

location.en: a place away from a studio where a film is shot

ex.ko: 그 영화는 이탈리아 현지 로케이션에서 촬영되었다.

lodge.en: a small house, especially in the mountains or countryside, used for a short stay

ex.en: We stayed at a ski lodge for the weekend.
ex.ko: 우리는 주말 동안 스키 산장에 묵었다.

lodge.en: formally submit a complaint or protest

log.en: a thick piece cut from the trunk of a tree

ex.en: They threw another log on the fire.
ex.ko: 그들은 불에 통나무를 하나 더 넣었다.

log.en: a written record of events, especially over time

logic.en: a way of thinking or reasoning that seems reasonable and correct

ex.en: There's no logic in his argument.

logo.en: a symbol or design that represents a company or brand

lorry.en: a large vehicle used for carrying goods (British English for truck)

loss.en: the fact of no longer having something you had before

loss.en: the sadness of losing someone you love, especially through death

ex.en: She is still coping with the loss of her father.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아직도 아버지를 잃은 상실감을 견디고 있다.

loss.en: the amount of money lost when costs are more than income

ex.en: The business reported a loss last quarter.
ex.ko: 그 사업체는 지난 분기에 적자를 기록했다.

lottery.en: a game in which people buy tickets and win prizes based on random numbers

ex.en: He won the lottery last year.

lottery.cue: 결과가 순전히 운에 달린 일을 비유
lottery.en: something whose outcome is entirely a matter of chance

ex.en: Finding a good apartment in this city is a lottery.
ex.ko: 이 도시에서 좋은 아파트를 구하는 건 순전히 운이다.

loud.en: producing a lot of noise; not quiet

ex.en: The music was too loud for me to sleep.
ex.ko: 그 음악은 너무 시끄러워서 나는 잠을 잘 수 없었다.

loud.en: with a loud voice or sound

ex.en: Please speak louder so everyone can hear.
ex.ko: 모두가 들을 수 있도록 더 크게 말해 주세요.

loudspeaker.en: a device that makes sound loud enough to be heard at a distance

ex.en: The announcement was made over the loudspeaker.
ex.ko: 그 안내 방송은 확성기를 통해 나왔다.

lovingly.en: in a way that shows love or care

ex.en: She lovingly wrapped the gift for her son.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아들을 위해 애정을 담아 선물을 포장했다.

loyal.en: faithful and supportive to a person, group, or cause

ex.en: He has been a loyal friend for years.

loyalty.en: strong support and faithfulness towards someone or something

ex.en: She showed great loyalty to her company.
ex.ko: 그녀는 회사에 대한 큰 충성심을 보였다.

luggage.en: the bags and suitcases you take when traveling

ex.en: We checked our luggage at the airport.

lung.en: one of the two organs in the chest used for breathing

ex.en: Smoking can seriously damage your lungs.
ex.ko: 흡연은 폐를 심각하게 손상시킬 수 있다.

luxury.en: something enjoyable that is not necessary, often expensive

magical.en: having a wonderful, mysterious quality, as if produced by magic

ex.en: The city looked magical under the snow.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 눈이 내리자 마법처럼 보였다.

magician.en: a person who performs magic tricks to entertain people

ex.en: The magician made the coin disappear.
The view from the mountain was magnificent.

mailbox.en: a box outside a house where mail is delivered

ex.en: He checked the mailbox for letters.

mailbox.en: a public box on the street where you post letters

ex.en: She dropped the letter in the mailbox on the corner.

main.en: the most important part or thing

ex.en: The main reason I came was to see you.
ex.ko: 내가 온 주된 이유는 너를 보기 위해서였다.

maintain.en: keep something in its current state or condition

ex.en: It's important to maintain a healthy diet.

maintain.en: state firmly that something is true

ex.en: He maintained that he was innocent.

maintain.en: keep a machine, building, or system in good working condition

ex.en: The company maintains its vehicles regularly.

maintenance.en: the work needed to keep something in good condition

ex.en: The building requires regular maintenance.

majority.en: more than half of a group or number

management.en: the process of controlling and organizing a business or activity

ex.en: Good time management is essential for students.

management.en: the people who manage or run a business

ex.en: The management decided to hire more staff.

mankind.en: all human beings considered as a group

ex.en: The invention changed the course of mankind.

maple.en: a tree with broad leaves that turn red or orange in autumn

ex.en: The maple trees turned bright red in October.

marble.en: a hard smooth stone used for building and sculpture

ex.en: The floor was made of white marble.
ex.ko: 그 바닥은 흰색 대리석으로 되어 있었다.

marble.en: a small ball of glass used in children's games

ex.en: The kids were playing marbles in the yard.
Marine life is abundant along this coast.
ex.ko: 이 해안을 따라 해양 생물이 풍부하다.

marine.en: a soldier trained to serve on both land and sea

ex.en: Her brother served as a marine for six years.
There was a marked improvement in her grades.

marriage.en: the state of being legally joined to someone as husband, wife, or partner

ex.en: They have a happy marriage.
ex.ko: 그들은 행복한 결혼 생활을 하고 있다.

marriage.en: the act or ceremony of getting married

ex.en: Their marriage took place in a small church.

mash.en: crush food into a soft mass by pressing it

ex.en: She mashed the potatoes with butter and milk.
ex.ko: 그녀는 버터와 우유를 넣어 감자를 으깼다.

mass.en: a large amount or number of something gathered together

ex.en: A mass of clouds covered the sky.

mass.en: the amount of physical matter an object has

ex.en: The mass of the object was measured in kilograms.
ex.ko: 그 물체의 질량은 킬로그램 단위로 측정되었다.

mass.en: involving a large number of people

ex.en: The concert drew mass attention from the media.
ex.ko: 그 콘서트는 언론의 대대적인 관심을 끌었다.

massive.en: very large in size, amount, or degree

ex.en: The company suffered a massive loss last year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 작년에 막대한 손실을 입었다.

mathematician.en: a person who is an expert in mathematics

ex.en: The mathematician solved the difficult equation.
ex.ko: 그 수학자는 그 어려운 방정식을 풀었다.

mathematics.en: the study of numbers, shapes, and quantities

ex.en: She teaches mathematics at the local high school.

maximum.en: the largest possible amount or degree

ex.en: The elevator has a maximum capacity of ten people.
ex.ko: 그 엘리베이터는 최대 10명까지 탈 수 있다.

maximum.en: the greatest possible in amount or degree

ex.en: The maximum speed on this road is 60 km/h.

mayor.en: the person elected to lead the government of a city or town

ex.en: The mayor announced a new plan for the city center.
ex.ko: 그 시장은 도심을 위한 새 계획을 발표했다.

meaningful.en: having a purpose or importance that is worth noting

ex.en: She wanted a more meaningful job.
ex.ko: 그녀는 더 의미 있는 일을 원했다.

meanwhile.en: during the time between two events, or at the same time as something else

ex.en: He was cooking dinner; meanwhile, she set the table.
ex.ko: 그는 저녁을 요리하고 있었고, 그동안 그녀는 식탁을 차렸다.

measure.en: find the size, amount, or degree of something using an instrument or standard

ex.en: She measured the length of the table.

measure.en: an action taken to achieve a particular goal or deal with a problem

ex.en: The government took measures to reduce pollution.
ex.ko: 정부는 오염을 줄이기 위한 조치를 취했다.

measurement.en: the size, length, or amount of something as found by measuring

ex.en: He took the measurements of the room before buying furniture.
ex.ko: 그는 가구를 사기 전에 방 치수를 쟀다.

mechanic.en: a person whose job is to repair machines, especially cars

ex.en: The mechanic fixed the car's engine.

mechanical.en: operated by or relating to machines

ex.en: The clock has a mechanical movement inside it.
ex.ko: 그 시계는 내부에 기계 장치가 있다.

meditate.en: sit quietly and focus your mind for relaxation or spiritual purposes

ex.en: She meditates for twenty minutes every morning.

meditation.en: the practice of focusing your mind quietly for relaxation or spiritual purposes

ex.en: He practices meditation to relieve stress.
ex.ko: 그는 스트레스를 풀기 위해 명상을 한다.

medium.en: neither large nor small; average in size or amount

ex.en: I'd like a medium coffee, please.

medium.en: a means by which something is communicated or expressed

ex.en: Television is a powerful medium for advertising.

melt.en: become or make something become liquid, usually by heating

ex.en: The ice cream melted quickly in the sun.

membership.en: the state of belonging to a club, group, or organization

ex.en: She renewed her gym membership.

memorable.en: worth remembering because it is special or important

ex.en: Our trip to Japan was truly memorable.
ex.ko: 우리의 일본 여행은 정말 기억에 남았다.

memorize.en: learn something so well that you can remember it exactly

ex.en: She memorized the whole poem for class.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수업을 위해 그 시 전체를 암기했다.

mend.en: repair something that is torn or broken

ex.en: She mended the hole in his sock.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그의 양말에 난 구멍을 수선했다.

mental.en: relating to the mind rather than the body

ex.en: Exercise is good for both physical and mental health.
ex.ko: 운동은 신체 건강과 정신 건강 모두에 좋다.

ex.en: She was mentally exhausted after the exam.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시험이 끝난 후 정신적으로 지쳤다.

merchant.en: a person who buys and sells goods, especially in large quantities

ex.en: The merchant sold silk and spices from the East.
ex.ko: 그 상인은 동방에서 온 비단과 향신료를 팔았다.

mere.en: used to emphasize how unimportant or small something is

ex.en: It took her a mere five minutes to finish.
I merely asked a simple question.
ex.ko: 나는 그저 간단한 질문을 했을 뿐이다.

mess.en: a state of being untidy or disorganized

ex.en: His room was a complete mess.
He got himself into a real mess with his finances.
ex.ko: 그는 재정 문제로 정말 곤란한 상황에 처했다.

message board.en: a board, often online, where people can post messages for others to read

ex.en: She posted her question on the school's message board.
The kitchen was messy after the party.

middle-aged.en: being roughly between 45 and 65 years old

ex.en: A middle-aged man was walking his dog in the park.
ex.ko: 한 중년 남성이 공원에서 개를 산책시키고 있었다.

mild.en: not strong, harsh, or severe in effect

ex.en: The winter was mild this year.

mild.en: (of food) not strong or spicy in flavor

ex.en: He prefers mild curry to spicy curry.
ex.ko: 그는 매운 카레보다 순한 카레를 더 좋아한다.

mile.en: a unit for measuring distance, equal to about 1.6 kilometers

ex.en: The store is about two miles from here.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 여기서 약 2마일 떨어져 있다.

mineral.en: a natural solid substance found in the earth, such as gold or salt

ex.en: This region is rich in minerals like iron and copper.
ex.ko: 이 지역은 철과 구리 같은 광물이 풍부하다.

minimize.en: reduce something to the smallest possible amount or degree

ex.en: Wearing a helmet helps minimize the risk of injury.
ex.ko: 헬멧을 쓰면 부상의 위험을 최소화하는 데 도움이 된다.

minimum.en: the smallest possible amount or degree

ex.en: You need a minimum of two years' experience for this job.
ex.ko: 이 일을 하려면 최소 2년의 경력이 필요하다.

minimum.en: the smallest possible in amount or degree

ex.en: The minimum age to vote is eighteen.
He suffered only minor injuries in the accident.
ex.ko: 그는 그 사고에서 경미한 부상만 입었다.

minor.en: a person who is under the legal age of adulthood

ex.en: It's illegal to sell alcohol to minors.

minority.cue: 전체 중 절반이 안 되는 수
minority.en: the smaller part of a group or number

ex.en: Only a minority of voters supported the plan.
ex.ko: 유권자 중 소수만이 그 계획을 지지했다.

minority.en: a group of people who differ in race, culture, or background from the larger population

ex.en: The policy aims to protect the rights of minorities.
ex.ko: 그 정책은 소수 집단의 권리를 보호하는 것을 목표로 한다.

minus.en: the arithmetic operation of subtracting one number from another

ex.en: Ten minus three equals seven.

minus.en: below zero or a negative amount

ex.en: It was minus five degrees this morning.

miracle.en: an amazing and unlikely event that seems impossible to explain

ex.en: It was a miracle that no one was hurt in the crash.
ex.ko: 그 충돌 사고에서 아무도 다치지 않은 것은 기적이었다.

mischief.en: playful behavior that causes minor trouble or annoyance

ex.en: The children were up to mischief in the garden.
She felt miserable after losing her job.
ex.ko: 그녀는 직장을 잃은 후 비참한 기분이 들었다.

misery.en: a state of great unhappiness or suffering

ex.en: The war brought misery to millions of people.
ex.ko: 그 전쟁은 수백만 명에게 고통을 안겨주었다.

mislead.en: give someone false or confusing information so that they believe something wrong

ex.en: The advertisement misled customers about the product's price.
ex.ko: 그 광고는 제품 가격에 대해 고객들을 오도했다.

mediterranean.en: relating to or near the Mediterranean Sea

ex.en: They enjoy a Mediterranean diet rich in olive oil and vegetables.
ex.ko: 그들은 올리브 오일과 채소가 풍부한 지중해식 식단을 즐긴다.

mediterranean.en: the sea between southern Europe, North Africa, and western Asia

ex.en: They sailed across the Mediterranean last summer.
